yaml-workflow-leading-comment-as-purpose.l0: CI workflow leading `#` comment = purpose metadata; headerComment() handles above/below name:, stops at first body key.
yaml-workflow-leading-comment-as-purpose.l1: PR #339: `headerComment()` in ci-schedules.js extracts a workflow's purpose from its leading `#` comment block. Handles two layouts: comment block appears ABOVE `name:` (common), or BELOW `name:` (before first body key). Blank comment lines skipped; scan stops at first non-comment body key. `parseCronWorkflows` now carries a `description` field. `graph-view.js ciRow` renders it as inline `sched-desc` label + `title=` tooltip. All 18 cron workflows expose a human-readable purpose in SCHEDULES — no separate purpose registry needed. Convention: new workflows must include a leading `#` block.
yaml-workflow-leading-comment-as-purpose.value:
Pattern: use leading `#` comment blocks in GitHub Actions workflow files as machine-readable purpose metadata.

(1) Placement rules: comments may appear ABOVE `name:` (most common) or BELOW `name:` but before the first body key (e.g. `on:`, `jobs:`). Blank comment lines within the block are skipped. Scan stops at the first non-comment, non-blank line.

(2) Parser (headerComment(content)):
  - Split on newlines.
  - Skip leading blank lines.
  - Collect lines starting with `#`; stop on the first non-comment, non-blank line.
  - If `name:` is the first non-comment line, reset and continue collecting the next `#` block.
  - Return joined non-empty comment text (strip leading `# ` prefix). Return '' when absent — never null.

(3) Integration: surface as a `description` field on each workflow object returned by `parseCronWorkflows`/`listCiSchedules`. Always a string; '' when absent.

(4) Rendering: inline `sched-desc` label for quick scanning + `title=` attribute tooltip for the full text. Mirrors the existing mesh `jobRow` pattern so both job types have consistent display.

(5) Convention: every new .github/workflows/*.yml file must begin with a descriptive `#` comment block (above or immediately below `name:`). This ensures SCHEDULES dashboard visibility and provides a single source of truth for human readers — no separate purpose registry to maintain.

(6) Complementary to ci-schedules-yaml-section-scope: that lesson skips `#` lines when PARSING cron expressions; this lesson READS `#` lines for purpose metadata. They target different fields in the same scan pass.

Provenance: PR #339 (2026-06-21).

provenance.source: PR #339: feat(dashboard): hover/inline purpose for each GitHub Action in SCHEDULES

mir-ci-artifact-sync-before-collect.l0: MIR blind on local: sync CI eval artifacts pre-collect; no-op when GITHUB_ACTIONS; degrade silently on gh/auth failure.
mir-ci-artifact-sync-before-collect.l1: PR #342: runMir reads eval-results/, adversarial-results/, perf-results/ — dirs only staged by CI. Local daemon never has them → all eval metrics null. Fix: sync-artifacts.js pulls gh run download (newest-run-first, first-hit-wins) BEFORE collectInputs. Guard 1: no-op when GITHUB_ACTIONS — fresh artifacts already staged; re-pulling overwrites with older run's data. Guard 2: per-artifact fail-safe — missing gh, no auth, expired artifact → degrade to null-metric; never aborts. Baseline/policy activate automatically once data flows; no schema changes needed.
mir-ci-artifact-sync-before-collect.value:
Apply when an analysis/reporting tool reads artifact directories populated only by CI (integration tests, eval scorecards, benchmark results) — and the same tool runs in a local daemon with no CI context.

(1) Sync step BEFORE collect: add a sync-artifacts.js (or equivalent) that calls gh run download for each artifact → local dir map, newest-run-first (list workflow runs by status=success, pick the newest, download once per artifact type). Place the sync BEFORE collectInputs so downstream code behaves identically whether data arrived from CI staging or from the sync.

(2) No-op on CI: guard with if (process.env.GITHUB_ACTIONS) — when running inside integration.yml, the current run's fresh artifacts are already staged by an earlier workflow step. Re-downloading would overwrite fresh results with an older run's data, corrupting the current scorecard.

(3) Per-artifact fail-safe: wrap each download in its own try/catch. Missing gh binary, no auth, rate-limited, or expired artifact (GitHub retention ≤90 days) → degrade to null/missing for that artifact only. Never throw from the sync step; never abort the report. The existing null-metric baseline behavior is the correct degraded state.

(4) Downstream activation is automatic: baseline.js trend-append and policy.js regression-filing both activate as soon as non-null data flows — no schema changes, no new flags needed. The sync is purely additive.

(5) Test pattern: inject a fake gh binary via spawnSync; cover artifact-to-dir map, newest-run-first selection, older-run fallback, GITHUB_ACTIONS no-op, empty results, and per-artifact failure isolation (one download error must not block others).

Provenance: PR #342 (2026-06-21), closes #337.

provenance.source: PR #342: feat(mir): sync CI eval scorecards into local MIR runs — un-blind quality metrics (#337)

discover-deploy-confidence-rank.l0: Discover agent folders by confidence (agent.json>system.md>AGENT.md); deploy is dry-run-default + name-idempotent.
discover-deploy-confidence-rank.l1: PR #344: before this, wiring an agent required knowing its path and running add --apply + doctor manually. discover does a bounded-depth walk (maxDepth=4 default), ranks folders by marker confidence: agent.json=high, prompts/system.md=medium, AGENT.md=low; matched folder = leaf. deploy: substrate → discover → add out-of-tree candidates → doctor. Name-idempotent (re-run skips registered agents). A folder under the mesh root is reported for join only. discover is read-only; AGENT.md content is detection-only, never executed.
discover-deploy-confidence-rank.value:
Pattern: confidence-ranked agent discovery + name-idempotent deploy for one-click mesh bootstrapping.

(1) discover (src/builder/discover.js): read-only bounded-depth walk (default maxDepth=4). Ranks candidate folders by marker confidence:
  - high: agent.json present
  - medium: prompts/system.md present
  - low: AGENT.md present
A matched folder is a leaf — no further descent. Prunes noise dirs (node_modules, .git, vendor, dist, build) and mesh-substrate dirs. Annotates alreadyInMesh against the manifest.

(2) deploy (src/builder/deploy.js): chains initMesh (if absent) → discover → add each new out-of-tree candidate → doctor.
  - Dry-run by default; --apply required to write.
  - Name-idempotent: re-deploy skips already-registered agents.
  - A folder UNDER the mesh root is reported for 'join' only, never added into itself.
  - One agent's add failure is captured in errors[] and never aborts the rest.

(3) Safety invariants preserved:
  - AGENT.md is detected for presence only — content never read or executed (AGENT.md-as-data invariant).
  - discover spawns nothing and writes nothing.
  - deploy mutations go through existing add/doctor/initMesh with their single-writable-root guards.

(4) CLI:
  - agent-mesh discover <scan-root> [--mesh <mesh-root>] [--depth N] [--json]
  - agent-mesh deploy <scan-root> --mesh <mesh-root> [--modes ask,do] [--depth N] [--apply]

(5) Smoke check: discover dev-mesh --mesh dev-mesh shows all agents as [in mesh]; deploy dev-mesh --mesh <tmp> (dry-run) plans a fresh mesh + all agents without writing.

Provenance: PR #344 (2026-06-21).

provenance.source: PR #344: feat(cli): discover + deploy — recognize and one-click-join local agents

agent-log-dir-per-agent-root.l0: Delegate run logs live per-agent under `<meshRoot>/<agent>/.agent-mesh/`; aggregators must enumerate agent subdirs.
agent-log-dir-per-agent-root.l1: PR #346: daily-report.mjs read delegate run logs from a single top-level `<repoRoot>/.agent-mesh/logs/` path. AGENT_MESH_LOG_DIR resolves under each *served* agent's root — real logs are at `<meshRoot>/analyst/.agent-mesh/logs/`, `<meshRoot>/tester/.agent-mesh/logs/`, etc. Top-level dir is empty → local.runs: 0 → all-zero tokens/cost in today/week/month dashboard panels. Fix: agentLogDirs() enumerates non-dot agent subdirs; readLocalLogsMulti() reads and deduplicates the started/done record pair per run-id (keep the usage-bearing done record).
agent-log-dir-per-agent-root.value:
Apply when adding any aggregation script that reads per-agent mesh run logs.

(1) Log dir is per-agent: AGENT_MESH_LOG_DIR resolves relative to each agent's own root folder (`<meshRoot>/<agent>/`). Never read from `<repoRoot>/.agent-mesh/logs/` — that path is empty in production. Correct helper:
  agentLogDirs({ meshRoot, logSeg = '.agent-mesh/logs', extraDirs = [] })
  → readdirSync(meshRoot), skip dot-dirs, return `path.join(subdir, logSeg)` for each. Pass extraDirs for legacy back-compat.

(2) Multi-dir read + dedup: each `delegate-<date>.jsonl` records two lines per run: a `started` line (no usage) and a `done` line (with tokens/cost). Concatenating N dirs double-counts. Dedup by `run.id`, keeping the done/usage-bearing record:
  dedupeRunRecords(records): for each id, keep the record with usage set; fall back to last-seen.

(3) meshRoot resolution: `process.env.DEV_SOCIETY_MESH_ROOT || path.join(repoRoot, 'dev-mesh')`.

(4) Dot-dir skip: skip `subdir.startsWith('.')` — `.git`, `.agent-mesh` are tool dirs, not agents.

(5) Test coverage: per-agent enumeration, dot-dir skip, missing meshRoot tolerance, cross-agent aggregation with started/done dedup, missing log file tolerance. PR #346 ships `test/report-per-agent-logs.test.js` (5 tests).

(6) Follow-up gap (not this PR): run records write `route: null` → per-route breakdown groups everything under 'unknown'. A separate delegate.js capture fix is needed; it does not affect total cost/token counts.

Provenance: PR #346 (2026-06-21).

provenance.source: PR #346: fix(report): token panel was always $0 — daily-report read the wrong (empty) log path

per-delegation-cost-budget-guard.l0: Delegation cost cap: AGENT_MESH_COST_BUDGET_USD; per-bridge accumulator; fan-out needs a separate pre-flight.
per-delegation-cost-budget-guard.l1: PR #351 spec: readCostBudget(env) → null|USD in src/config.js; in RESERVED_BRIDGE_ENV (peer.env cannot override). Accumulator: per-bridge-process closure var accumulatedCostUsd=0, resets per claude -p (same scope as depth/path). error_code: cost_budget_exceeded, same structured-refusal shape. fanOutToPeers bypasses delegateToPeer → must get its own pre-flight guard (separate from delegateToPeer's). Accumulate subtree_cost_usd post-settle even on child error (null → skip). Risk: concurrent fan-out may overshoot by one child's cost; timeout paths may under-count.
per-delegation-cost-budget-guard.value:
Implement per-delegation cost governance via a per-bridge accumulator in peer-bridge.js.

(1) CONFIG: AGENT_MESH_COST_BUDGET_USD (string; unset → no guard). Parse in src/config.js:
  readCostBudget(env): const v = Number(env.AGENT_MESH_COST_BUDGET_USD);
  return Number.isFinite(v) && v > 0 ? v : null;
  Add to RESERVED_BRIDGE_ENV so registry.json peer.env cannot override (anti-spoof invariant).

(2) ACCUMULATOR: in createBridge() closure:
  const costBudget = readCostBudget(env);   // null → disabled
  let accumulatedCostUsd = 0;               // resets at process exit (per claude -p span)

(3) PRE-FLIGHT in delegateToPeer (after depth check, before spawn):
  if (costBudget !== null && accumulatedCostUsd >= costBudget)
    return refuseLogged('cost_budget_exceeded', ...);
  Uses >= (hitting the limit also refuses — safe, not permissive).

(4) SEPARATE PRE-FLIGHT in fanOutToPeers: fanOutToPeers dispatches via Promise.allSettled WITHOUT calling delegateToPeer, so the delegateToPeer guard never fires. Add an identical check inside fanOutToPeers after its depth pre-flight.

(5) ACCUMULATION post-settle (in BOTH sites):
  if (typeof mapped.subtree_cost_usd === 'number') accumulatedCostUsd += mapped.subtree_cost_usd;
  Accumulate even on child error/timeout (tokens burned = real cost). null subtree_cost_usd → skip silently.

(6) RISKS:
  - Fan-out overshoot: all fan-out peers dispatched before any settle; pre-flight is a single aggregate gate. A concurrent delegateToPeer completing between fan-out pre-flight and settle can cause slight over-run. Bounded by FAN_OUT_MAX_PEERS; set budget with headroom.
  - Timeout under-count: if child times out before JSON envelope is written, subtree_cost_usd is null → not accumulated. Best-effort heuristic; timeout paths are already structured failures.
  - Per-session scope: multi-turn interactive sessions do NOT accumulate across turns (each claude -p spawns a fresh bridge).

(7) TESTS (test/peer-bridge.test.js): no-budget env → disabled; budget=0.10, accumulated=0 → passes; accumulated=0.10 → refused; peer.env budget → RESERVED_BRIDGE_ENV blocks; child error → cost still accumulated; fan-out refused when exhausted.

Provenance: PR #351 spec (2026-06-21), closes idea #350.

provenance.source: PR #351: spec: idea: per-delegation cost budget guard — prevent unbounded subtree spend via bridge circuit-breaker (#350)

mir-slow-drift-trend-gate.l0: MIR rolling median tracks slow drift — add Q1 two-half trend check as orthogonal signal for gradual deterioration.
mir-slow-drift-trend-gate.l1: PR #352 spec: when a metric degrades gradually across many runs, the rolling-window median baseline degrades in sync — individual runs stay within the noise band and no finding ever fires. Fix: additive trend check — split history into oldest/newest halves, compute Q1 of each, classify direction via metric-direction map. Adverse AND magnitude > noiseBandPct → emit 'trend:<metric>:<cell>' (cluster: trend-regression). Distinct id from per-run finding — both can coexist. Reuses AGENT_MESH_MIR_TREND_N + AGENT_MESH_MIR_NOISE_BAND_PCT; no new env vars.
mir-slow-drift-trend-gate.value:
Apply when adding trend monitoring on top of a rolling-window median baseline: the median solves per-run noise but is susceptible to gradual drift — the baseline degrades with the metric, so individual runs never breach the noise band.

Algorithm (additive — runs alongside per-run check, does not replace it):
1. Require ≥ TREND_N data points (default 10). Short history → skip.
2. Split window into oldest and newest halves.
3. Compute Q1 (first quartile) of each half — one outlier run cannot flip a half's verdict (unlike mean/min).
4. Classify direction using the existing metric-direction map ('higher-is-better' vs 'lower-is-better').
5. Adverse AND |magnitude| > noiseBandPct → emit finding id 'trend:<metric>:<cell>', cluster 'trend-regression'.

Finding lifecycle via existing planIssues path (no new code):
- First appearance → file issue
- Recurrence → update
- Reversal (drift no longer adverse) → close

Id 'trend:<metric>:<cell>' is distinct from the per-run id so both can coexist when a metric has both a spike and a long-term drift.

Config reuse: AGENT_MESH_MIR_TREND_N and AGENT_MESH_MIR_NOISE_BAND_PCT — no new env vars.

Noop cases: history < TREND_N; flat trend (oldest Q1 ≈ newest Q1); favorable drift (metric improving).

Complementary to mir-rolling-window-median-baseline (that lesson solves per-run noise; this lesson closes the blind spot the median creates for slow cumulative regressions that never breach the noise band individually). Provenance: PR #352 / Issue #349 (2026-06-21).

provenance.source: PR #352: spec: idea: MIR slow-drift trend gate — rolling-window median masks gradual metric deterioration (#349)
